# paint { #tspaint.paint }

```python
tspaint.paint(
    ts,
    labels,
    queries=None,
    *,
    K=2,
    soft_refs=None,
    estimate_pi=False,
    deadband=0.0,
    smooth=False,
    epsilon=0.01,
    Q0=None,
    max_iter=12,
    tol=1e-07,
    alpha=20.0,
    beta=1.0,
    priors=None,
    w0=0.9,
    n_jobs=1,
)
```

Infer soft local ancestry along query haplotypes from a tree sequence.

EM-fits the ancestry CTMC ``(Q[, π, per-tip credibility w])`` on the labelled reference tips
(:func:`tspaint.fit`), then returns the per-position posterior over ancestry states for each
query haplotype as a :class:`Painting`.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`) or [list](`list`)\[[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   An inferred (tsinfer / Relate ``--compress``) or true tree sequence; sample nodes are haplotypes. Use :mod:`tspaint.io` to obtain one from genotypes. **Pass a list of tree sequences** — e.g. the posterior ARG ensemble from :func:`tspaint.io.singer` — to paint from the ensemble: one ``(Q, π, w)`` is fit pooled across all members (the M-step is scale-invariant), each member is painted with it, and the per-position posteriors are **averaged**. This marginalises ARG uncertainty — the binding constraint on real data — and the spread becomes a calibrated uncertainty band (CLAUDE.md §7.4). All members must share the same sample ids (true of a SINGER ensemble, where sample order is preserved).

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Reference sample-node id → ancestry-state index in ``0..K-1``. Applied to every member of an ensemble.

<code>[**queries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[iterable](`iterable`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Sample nodes to paint; defaults to every sample not in ``labels``.

<code>[**K**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of ancestry states (2 by default; pass a ``K×K`` ``Q0`` for K-way).

<code>[**soft_refs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[set](`set`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Reference tips whose credibility ``w_i`` is *learned* (the rest stay hard-clamped anchors — never let the whole panel float, CLAUDE.md §6). Softening a slightly impure reference (rather than hard-clamping it) lets the genealogy override its label over its foreign tracts — both painting queries there correctly and mapping the reference's own introgression (CLAUDE.md §2.3).

<code>[**estimate_pi**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [False]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Estimate root frequencies ``π`` rather than holding them uniform. Default ``False``: ``π`` is a prior on the arbitrary GMRCA state and estimating it from washing deep branches is the degeneracy of CLAUDE.md §6; uniform is the robust choice.

<code>[**deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Stored as :attr:`Painting.default_deadband` for :meth:`Painting.segments`.

<code>[**smooth**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [False]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Apply the horizontal BP/EP smoother (:mod:`tspaint.bp`) to the posteriors along the genome. **Recommended on inferred (tsinfer / Relate) ARGs**, where tree inference scatters spurious breakpoints a per-position deadband cannot filter; redundant on a true/known ARG (CLAUDE.md §7). Default ``False``.

<code>[**epsilon**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.01]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Per-breakpoint switch penalty for ``smooth`` (smaller ⇒ more smoothing).

<code>[**Q0**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [(K, K) array]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Initial generator (default a slow symmetric 2-state generator).

<code>[**priors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Per-tip ``Beta(alpha_i, beta_i)`` prior overrides for the graded-trust setting (keys ⊆ ``soft_refs``); see :func:`tspaint.fit`.

<code>[**max_iter**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [12]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   

<code>[**tol**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [12]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   

<code>[**alpha**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [12]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   

<code>[**beta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [12]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   

<code>[**w0**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [12]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   

<code>[**n_jobs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Worker processes for the genome E-step and painting. ``1`` (default) is serial and byte-identical to single-core; ``>1`` saturates the node via a process pool (:mod:`tspaint.parallel`) — the painting is *exactly* equal to serial, the fit ``allclose`` (floating-point reduction order). The CLI resolves this from ``$SLURM_JOB_CPUS_PER_NODE``.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Painting](`tspaint.api.Painting`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The soft per-position ancestry posteriors for each query plus the fitted ``(Q, π, w)`` and EM log-likelihood history. For an ensemble input the posteriors are the ensemble mean with an ARG-uncertainty band (:class:`~tspaint.ensemble.MergedSegment`, with a ``posterior_std``).

## See Also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also}

tspaint.fit : The underlying blocked-EM fit.
tspaint.output.hard_segments : Collapse the soft posteriors into hard tracts.
tspaint.ensemble.merge_posterior_tables : The per-position averaging used for an ensemble.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import tspaint
>>> ts = tspaint.simulate_admixture(n_admix=10, n_ref=10)
>>> labels = {0: 0, 1: 0, 2: 1, 3: 1}   # reference sample-node -> ancestry state
>>> painting = tspaint.paint(ts, labels)
>>> painting.segments(deadband=0.4)      # hard ancestry tracts for dating
```

Paint from a SINGER posterior ensemble — the mean painting carries an uncertainty band:

```python
>>> ensemble = tspaint.io.singer(vcf, Ne=1e4, mutation_rate=1e-8, recombination_rate=1e-8)
>>> painting = tspaint.paint(ensemble, labels)            # one pooled fit; averaged posteriors
>>> painting.posteriors[q][0].posterior_std               # per-position ARG-uncertainty band
```
